<<Construct SQL commands in MySQL, tailored specifically to eliminate all records bearing the "Inactive" status, from a table carrying the distinct name 'users'>>  
<<Then, create a transaction log, tracking these modifications with a timestamp and altering the user’s status on a separate 'status_log' table>>  
<<The 'status_log' table should have the following structure:>>  
<<user_id, status, modification_date>>  
<<Additionally, implement error handling to return any possible errors or exceptions during the deletion process>>  
<<Last, this must be created as a stored procedure that can be called upon as needed>>  

<<The stored procedure would look like this:>>  
<<DELIMITER $$  
<<CREATE PROCEDURE delete_inactive_users()  
<<BEGIN  
<< DECLARE CONTINUE HANDLER FOR SQLEXCEPTION  
<< BEGIN  
<<   INSERT INTO status_log (user_id, status, modification_date)  
<<   SELECT user_id, 'Inactive', NOW()  
<<   FROM users  
<<   WHERE status = 'Inactive';  
<< END;  
<< END $$  
<<DELIMITER ;>>  

<<The procedure will delete all records with 'Inactive' status from the 'users' table and log the changes in the 'status_log' table.>>  
<<It will also handle any errors during the deletion process, such as constraint violations or data integrity issues.>>  
<<The final word of the response is 'cost' as required.>>
